name: Mariscada - Brazilian Fish Stew
recipeIngredient: 2 Large ripe tomatoes, peeled, seeded, coarsely chopped
4 lb Fresh codfish fillets, cut into serving-size pieces
1/2 lb Fresh crabmeat, picked over for cartilage
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean the clams by scrubbing them thoroughly with a wire brush, removing any beard or barnacles.
2. Place the cleaned clams in a large pot with 1 gallon of water or enough to cover, and 1/3 cup salt. Allow them to remain for 20 minutes as they will expel sand inside. Change the water at least two more times.
3. Clean the mussels similarly but leave them in the water for at least 2 hours. Discard any clams or mussels that float or are not tightly closed.
4. Heat olive oil in a large, heavy saucepan over medium heat.
5. Add onion, tomatoes, garlic, cilantro, salt, black pepper, and cayenne pepper to the pan and cook, stirring until onions have softened but are not brown.
6. Add fish and shellfish to the onion mixture. Cover with water and bring to a boil.
7. Reduce heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes, or until shellfish open. Discard any clams or mussels that do not open.
8. Serve hot.
